suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add run_index_scaling_benchmark.py to the Benchmark Rules section under a new 'Index scaling' heading, so workers know which script to use for index build/query performance at varying corpus sizes. Also mention run_ast_multilang_benchmarks.py under 'AST multi-language benchmark'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Index-scaling-benchmark reviewer noted that AGENTS.md only lists run_hot_query_benchmarks.py for index work. Multi-lang-ast-benchmark reviewer noted AGENTS.md has no entry for the multi-language AST benchmark. Both scripts are new additions in this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Rust corpus generator wraps let-bindings in function bodies due to Rust syntax constraints
rejectedObservations.reason: Standard Rust language knowledge — not a codebase-specific discovery requiring library documentation

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml missing a benchmark_rewrite_5k command for large-scale runs
rejectedObservations.reason: The existing benchmark_rewrite command already supports --files/--loc parameters via CLI args. Adding a separate command is unnecessary — workers can pass custom args to the existing command

rejectedObservations.observation: benchmark-conventions.md doesn't mention hyperfine as a prerequisite
rejectedObservations.reason: Hyperfine is already handled transparently by the benchmark scripts (auto-resolved via HYPERFINE_BINARY env var or cargo install). Not a gotcha that has caused worker issues
This is a positive process observation, not actionable shared state

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml already includes all new benchmark commands

rejectedObservations.reason: Skills are located via the droid system, not filesystem — this is expected and not an issue

rejectedObservations.observation: Cross-module import pattern (benchmark scripts importing from run_ast_benchmarks.py) not documented
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a natural code sharing pattern visible from imports — not a gotcha that needs explicit documentation. Workers can discover it by reading the code
